8. 🤬 Steerable Convolutions

We’ve built group convolution using the regular representation. Now we change basis into irreps. Again we’ll start by considering group features only and then spatial + group features.

8.1. Group only

Recall when we worked in the irrep basis for an abelian group we got the Fourier transform / convolution theorem:

(41)

and since the irreps are all 1D, the rep is diagonal in the irrep basis and so it becomes pointwise multiplication.

When the group is non-abelian, the irreps are multi-dimensional, so the quantity in Equation 41> is no longer pointwise multiplication.

[TODO] For non-abelian groups, in the irrep basis you can form complete outer program across all irrep compononets. Decomposing these outer products into irreps reveals that only the components transforming as the trivial representation survive. The same outer-product-then-select-trivial structure applies to any group, finite or continuous — and is the foundation of steerable equivariant neural networks.

8.2. [TODO] Spatial basis functions

With a group convolution, the filter lives in and equivriance is enforced by acting as a coupling tensor. Decomposing this tensor product into irreps reveals 72 scalar weights times basis elements (Schur’s lemma). Big picture: The irrep basis makes the free parameters manifest — they’re exactly the scalars, nothing more, nothing less. You’re not learning a filter and hoping it’s equivariant; you’re learning scalar amplitudes of patterns that are already equivariant by construction.

We additionally decompose the spatial structure — the result is a set of symmetry-adapted spatial basis functions for the filter, each labeled by an irrep. This way the permutation matrix becomes block diagonal (the group elements permuting the pixels admits a representation).

These turn out to be discrete samples of the polynomial in the character table.

8.3. Steerable Convolution Layer

Steerable convolutino layer, adapted from the tensor field network formulation is

8.4. Steerable Filters

A steerable filter is a scalar linear combination of spatial basis functions

Remark on course implementation of channels and connectivity

8.5. Activation Functions

Mixes within irreps as opposed to stud permuting group valued stuff (group convolution).

8.5.1. Norm-based activation

Norm of an irrep feature vector is invariant under the group action, so we can safely apply any nonlinearity to then norm then rescale.

8.5.2. Gated Activation

Gate each non-trivial irrep with a learned scalar from the trivial representation.

8.6. Computational Trade-Off

For larger/continuous groups, the regular rep becomes intractable. The steerable approach scales by working with a finite set of irreps rather than the whole group.
